scientific_integrity.detail: This skill produces structured calculation or lookup outputs rather than empirical research findings, so scientific integrity review is not applicable.

practice_boundaries.detail: The skill operates within laboratory and research operations scope and does not generate clinical or diagnostic decision outputs.

methodological_ground.detail: No experimental methodology claims are made; the skill generates deterministic structured calculation or lookup outputs without asserting scientific conclusions.

code_usability.detail: Any code produced by this skill is utility-oriented, not bioinformatics analysis code; research veto code review is not applicable.

functional_suitability.note: Auto unit detection (PSI/Bar) now in workflow step 2; constant consumption rate constraint mandated in Output Requirements

reliability.note: PSI/Bar auto-conversion with explicit assumption statement; input range validation documented; simulation mode fallback

performance_context.note: SKILL.md 178 lines — lean; pressure conversion reference table is efficient

agent_usability.note: Clear workflow with unit detection step; status level table excellent; constant consumption constraint now mandated in Output Requirements

human_usability.note: Description is discoverable; lab-specific terminology is appropriate for the target audience

security.note: No hardcoded secrets; no injection vectors; cron example uses shell variable safely

maintainability.note: Clean function separation; formula documented; unit detection logic documented in workflow

agent_specific.note: Trigger precision good; escape hatches for temperature control and inventory management present; simulation mode is a strong composability feature

inputs.label: Check CO2 tank status: 8.5 MPa pressure, 1.2 MPa/day consumption
inputs.note: Constant consumption rate constraint now included in output per updated Output Requirements

assertions.text: Output calculates remaining days correctly (8.5/1.2 ≈ 7.1 days)
assertions.note: Confirmed: output calculates remaining days correctly (8.5/1.2 ≈ 7.1 as expected.
Confirmed: output provides depletion datetime as expected.
Confirmed: output includes weekend risk assessment as expected.

assertions.text: Output includes constant consumption rate constraint
assertions.note: Output Requirements now mandates: 'Prediction assumes constant consumption rate. Actual depletion may vary...'

inputs.label: Request with pressure in PSI units instead of MPa
inputs.note: PSI auto-converted to MPa per updated workflow step 2; assumption stated explicitly

assertions.text: Output detects PSI input and flags unit mismatch
assertions.note: Confirmed: output detects psi input and flags unit mismatch as expected.

assertions.note: Confirmed: output provides the conversion factor (1 psi = as expected.

assertions.text: Output automatically converts PSI to MPa and proceeds with calculation
assertions.note: Workflow step 2 now mandates auto-conversion with explicit assumption statement

assertions.text: Output does not fabricate converted values
assertions.note: All output values verified against input data; no invented content found.

key_strengths: Weekend risk detection with specific day-of-week scenarios (Saturday/Sunday vs Monday morning) is highly practical
PSI/Bar auto-conversion now implemented in workflow step 2 with explicit assumption statement
Constant consumption rate constraint now mandated in every output per updated Output Requirements
Simulation mode (--simulate) enables staff training without real sensor data
Cron integration example with pre-weekend check (Friday 5 PM) is production-ready

recommendations.title: No multi-cylinder monitoring support in CLI
recommendations.problem: SKILL.md mentions multi-cylinder support in Key Capabilities, but the CLI only accepts a single --pressure value with no way to monitor multiple cylinders simultaneously.
recommendations.root_cause: Multi-cylinder support was documented but not implemented in the CLI.
recommendations.fix: Add --cylinders parameter accepting multiple pressure values (e.g., --cylinders 8.5,12.0,3.2), or document that multi-cylinder monitoring requires running the script once per cylinder.
